entries.title: League Of Villains Begins Small But Symbolic

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The early League of Villains is not yet a massive army, but its attack on U.A. gives it symbolic threat value.
content.injection: Before later villain expansion, portray the League as dangerous, ideologically disruptive, and backed by hidden support rather than numerically dominant.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

entries.title: Shigaraki Is An Immature Leader Early

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Early Tomura Shigaraki is dangerous but emotionally immature, impatient, and dependent on hidden support.
content.injection: Early Shigaraki should feel volatile and underdeveloped as a leader, not yet the evolved ideological center he becomes later.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

content.fact: Kurogiri's Warp Gate makes the early League far more operationally dangerous than its numbers imply.
content.injection: If Kurogiri is available, account for infiltration, extraction, and battlefield separation as core League tactics.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

entries.title: U.A. Faculty Are A Defensive Faction

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: During attacks on U.A., faculty operate as a protective pro-hero force around students and civilians.
content.injection: U.A. faculty should respond as trained heroes, prioritizing containment, evacuation, and student survival.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

entries.title: Class 1-A Is Not A Military Unit

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Class 1-A can act bravely under pressure, but early students are not a formal combat squad with mature command structure.
content.injection: Do not write early Class 1-A as a polished tactical military team; their cooperation should still be improvised and developing.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

entries.title: Police Depend On Hero Support Against Quirks

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Police remain legally important, but many high-risk Quirk incidents require licensed heroes for practical intervention.
content.injection: Crime scenes should include the division of labor between police procedure and hero capability.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

content.fact: Agencies connect students, pros, mentors, sidekicks, public image, and future opportunities.
content.injection: Use agencies as social and career infrastructure whenever internships or professional paths arise.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.

content.fact: Villain factions can recruit or manipulate people who feel abandoned, stigmatized, or harmed by hero society.
content.injection: Villain motivation must not be reduced to random evil when systemic alienation or manipulation is relevant.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

content.fact: The Hero Public Safety Commission can prioritize stability, control, and public order even when individual heroes disagree.
content.injection: Commission involvement should introduce institutional priorities beyond classroom, agency, or personal morality.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.

entries.title: Hero Society Treats U.A. As A Symbol

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: U.A. is not only a school; it is a public symbol of future hero society and therefore a target for villains and media.
content.injection: Threats to U.A. should matter publicly because they imply the system cannot protect its own future heroes.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

content.fact: Nomu are engineered weapons rather than ordinary recruits, and their behavior should be more limited and mission-driven.
content.injection: If a Nomu appears, write it as a constructed combat asset with specific capacities, not as a normal criminal with social motives.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

entries.title: The League Targets Symbolic Weak Points

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The early League seeks attacks that undermine confidence in heroes, especially U.A. and All Might.
content.injection: League plots should carry symbolic logic: exposing weakness matters as much as immediate casualties.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

content.fact: A small visible villain group may have resources, planning, or sponsors that students and teachers cannot yet see.
content.injection: Do not assume the visible villains on scene are the whole threat if their logistics imply backing.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.
antiLore: Do not apply later-war faction scale or competence to early arcs unless the story explicitly diverges.

content.fact: Villains, pros, media, and schools may view U.A. students as future symbols or assets, not just children in classes.
content.injection: When Class 1-A becomes public, remember that attention to them can be strategic, predatory, or reputational.
constraints: Preserve faction scale and maturity according to current Context.

content.injection: Use uneven protection to explain resentment, vulnerability, and villain recruitment without making every hero malicious.
